summary: Frontend e2e of the NEW Membership Auto-Enrol feature on top of SendReceiptModal. Ran the full 9-scenario flow (login cashier/1234 -> shift 500000 -> scan 8991111000011 -> F5 -> cash 10000 -> Receipt -> Send Receipt). Scenarios 1-3 PASS: empty input shows slate '— Masukkan nomor untuk auto-enrol membership —', existing phone 081234567890 -> GREEN pill 'Member M-0001 — BUDI SANTOSO', new phone 08570000123 -> AMBER 'Belum terdaftar. Akan otomatis didaftarkan saat dikirim.' Scenario 4 PARTIAL: clicking send-wa-link-btn with new phone fires sonner toast 'Member baru: M-0005 — PELANGGAN 0123' (creation works, observed in green-success toast on screenshot). HOWEVER the re-lookup of the SAME phone '08570000123' immediately returns AMBER 'Belum terdaftar' instead of GREEN with M-0005. This is a phone-normalization mismatch bug — see RCA. As a consequence Scenarios 5 (trx.customer.memberNumber verify), 6 (enrol OFF), 7 (EMAIL mailto), 8 (API path + re-lookup) and the WA regression were NOT executed because the test halted on the failed S4 re-lookup assertion.

integration_issues.flow: Membership auto-enrol via WhatsApp (phone) path -> re-lookup after creation
integration_issues.issue: After auto-enrol creates a new member from a WA phone (e.g. cashier types '08570000123', clicks send-wa-link-btn, toast shows 'Member baru: M-0005 — PELANGGAN 0123'), retyping the SAME phone '08570000123' into send-to-input does NOT find the just-created member: the send-member-status pill stays AMBER 'Belum terdaftar' instead of turning GREEN with 'Member M-0005'. Root cause is a phone-format mismatch between WRITE and READ paths in mockApi.js — see RCA. Existing seeded members M-0001..M-0004 (whose phone field stores raw '081234567890') still look up correctly; only NEW auto-enrolled members suffer because they are written as '+628570000123'.

design_issues.screen: SendReceiptModal -> Preview pesan / wa.me text body
issues: wa.me text begins with ~9 leading spaces (URL-decoded body starts with '         TOKO MAJU JAYA') because renderReceiptText centre-pads the store-name header. Cosmetic, not blocking, but the receipt header looks oddly indented inside WhatsApp's monospace preview.

design_issues.screen: SendReceiptModal -> auto-enrol amber pill on small widths
issues: The amber 'Belum terdaftar. Akan otomatis didaftarkan saat dikirim.' wraps with awkward justification (see screenshot — 'Akan otomatis didaftarkan saat dikirim.' wraps onto two lines with the 'Belum terdaftar.' tagline sitting next to 'Akan ...'). Consider flex-col on narrow modals or shortening the copy.

action_items: FIX phone-normalization mismatch in /app/frontend/src/services/mockApi.js. Two equivalent fixes — pick ONE: (A) In upsertMemberFromContact, store phone in a SINGLE canonical form (recommend: digits only, e.g. '628570000123', so it matches what `lookupCustomer` will read after normalization). Then update lookupCustomer to also strip '+' and leading '0->62' on the query before matching. (B) Easier: change lookupCustomer's phone branch to compare DIGITS ONLY on both sides — replace `db.customers.where('phone').equals(q).first()` with `db.customers.filter(c => (c.phone||'').replace(/[^\d]/g,'').replace(/^0/,'62') === q.replace(/[^\d]/g,'').replace(/^0/,'62')).first()`. This makes '08570000123', '+628570000123', '628570000123' and '0085700001230' all match the same record. The same digits-only comparison should be added inside upsertMemberFromContact's 'existing' check (currently it normalizes via `.replace(/[^\d]/g,'')` only — does NOT strip the leading-0 vs 62 prefix difference, so two SUCCESSIVE auto-enrols of '08570000123' WOULD create two different records the first time and the second time, because the first writes '+628570000123', whose digits-only is '628570000123', and the second writes the same so it would actually match on the 2nd call only — but the cashier-typed re-lookup with '08570000123' digits-only = '08570000123' still won't match '628570000123'. Bottom line: every place that compares phones needs the SAME normalize-to-(strip-+, leading-0->62) helper.)
After applying (B): re-run the full 9 scenarios. Scenarios 5/6/7/8 + regression were blocked by this bug and need re-verification.
(LOW / optional) Trim or left-align the receipt text used in wa.me / mailto body so the WhatsApp preview doesn't start with 9 leading spaces.
critical_code_review_comments: BUG: mockApi.upsertMemberFromContact stores `phone: phone || ''` where `phone` is `+628570000123` (built by SendReceiptModal). But mockApi.lookupCustomer's phone branch does `db.customers.where('phone').equals(q).first()` where q is the raw cashier input '08570000123'. These can NEVER match for newly auto-enrolled members. Existing seeded customers (M-0001 phone='081234567890') happen to match because the cashier types the exact same string. Fix described in action_items.

DRY violation: phone normalization logic is duplicated (SendReceiptModal's normalizedPhone, upsertMemberFromContact's `phoneNorm = phone.replace(/[^\d]/g,'')`, lookupCustomer's equality). Extract a single `normalizePhoneE164ish(s)` helper in /app/frontend/src/lib/format.js and use it in all 3 sites.

Idempotency caveat: even after fixing the lookup, the AUTO-ENROL itself is only idempotent if upsertMemberFromContact's `existing` probe also strips '+' and applies leading-0->62. Currently it strips non-digits only, so '+628570000123' (62...) vs '08570000123' (08...) are treated as different — clicking send twice with the same cashier-typed phone could create 2 customers if the toggle is left on. Apply the same canonical helper here.

Minor: mockApi.lookupCustomer first does `where('memberNumber').equals(q)` then `where('phone').equals(q)` — both rely on Dexie compound key types. The Dexie schema must declare `phone` as an indexed property; otherwise `.where('phone')` silently degrades. Verify db/database.js schema includes phone in the customers index list.

SendReceiptModal.jsx line 84-86: `name: trx?.customer?.name` is read but the *just-completed* trx may not yet have customer attached (which is exactly the scenario this feature targets). The fallback in upsertMemberFromContact `'PELANGGAN ${last4}'` saves us — good defensive default. Worth a one-line comment.

Test stability: send-member-status pill uses className-based color detection ('emerald' / 'amber' / 'slate' substring). This is brittle. Consider adding `data-state="green|amber|slate"` so future tests don't depend on Tailwind class strings.
Carry-over (LOW from iter 4): Radix DialogContent a11y warning (missing DialogDescription) — still emitted by SendReceiptModal. Cosmetic console-only.

Receipt text whitespace (cosmetic): renderReceiptText centre-pads the header lines with leading spaces; when piped into wa.me?text=..., the WhatsApp preview shows '         TOKO MAJU JAYA'. Consider trimStart()-ing each line before urlencode.

success_rate.frontend: 3.5 / 9 scenarios (S1, S2, S3 PASS; S4 partial — create works, re-lookup FAILS; S5-S8 + regression NOT EXECUTED due to halt on S4)

test_credentials: cashier / 1234 (verified working). Seeded customer M-0001 phone 081234567890 BUDI SANTOSO verified present in IndexedDB after fresh seed.
seed_data_creation: IndexedDB + localStorage cleared at start. seedIfEmpty repopulates 4 customers M-0001..M-0004, products + users. Then test FLOW creates M-0005 via auto-enrol (PELANGGAN 0123 phone +628570000123) — visible in 'Member baru' sonner toast.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent applies the phone-normalization fix in mockApi.js (action_items option B is the smallest patch), re-run all 9 scenarios from the review_request. Use these exact testids: login-username-input, login-pin-input, login-submit-btn, shift-opening-cash-input, shift-open-btn, pos-scan-input, payment-cash-input, payment-confirm-btn, receipt-send-btn, send-to-input, send-member-status, send-enrol-toggle, send-wa-link-btn, send-mailto-btn, send-api-btn, send-channel-email, send-channel-whatsapp. Pill color detection uses className substring ('emerald'/'amber'/'slate'). For wa.me / mailto clicks set `page.on('popup', lambda p: asyncio.create_task(p.close()))` to swallow popups. Member auto-enrol toast comes via sonner — query with `document.querySelectorAll('[data-sonner-toast], li, ol li')` and grep '/Member baru/' on textContent. IndexedDB name is `retail_pos_db`, transactions store contains the just-completed trx with `customer` field — verify customer.memberNumber === newly-created M-XXXX. Watch out: a fresh Playwright session re-seeds 4 customers and wipes any test-created M-0005+, so ALL assertions must run inside a SINGLE script run, not split across runs.
